[2018-02-25 13:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Are there any shortcuts you can take while calculating both defense vs freeze WHILE wanting to keep certain units in the defense alive?
[2018-02-25 13:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): For wanting to keep certain units in the defense alive, I just add their health to the opponent's damage
[2018-02-25 13:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But when freeze is involved, that won't necessarily work
[2018-02-25 13:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Because if they freeze the units whose health you added to the opponent's damage, you will get a fake damage amount that would indicate that they can breach, while they can't
[2018-02-25 13:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Surely you don't have to calculate all their freeze options that involve the units you don't want to lose though?
[2018-02-25 13:44] punf: if you have a blocking 1hp colossus that youre definitely not going to sac, just declare its not a blocker, and subtract 1 from your defence. how hard is that?
[2018-02-25 13:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Ok but if it's frozen, then it's not part of your defense, but they lose x freeze
[2018-02-25 13:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So you wouldn't lose it in that case, but they lost damage
[2018-02-25 13:45] punf: why would they freeze something that you dont want to lose
[2018-02-25 13:45] punf: you must play against worse opponents than i do
[2018-02-25 13:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well 1 hp Colossus is a bad example
[2018-02-25 13:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In the example I screenshot, 2nd Bite can only get 2 damage threat by freezing a Proto
[2018-02-25 13:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So if it does that, opponent threatens 1 extra damage
[2018-02-25 13:46] punf: that 1hp protoplasm isnt a blocker
[2018-02-25 13:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But one of the units you don't want to lose doesn't have to be removed from your defense calculations yet it won't be lost
[2018-02-25 13:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Lemme show actual numbers
[2018-02-25 13:46] punf: tbh: if youre playing a 45s game, then just calculate it. if youre playing a 6s game, just intuit it
[2018-02-25 13:46] punf: what time control do you want advice on? 20s?
[2018-02-25 13:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So in above example, top has 19 health on defense. 5 of that they don't want to lose.

Opponent has 10 attack, and 2x3 freeze. Freeze threatens 4 if it freezes a Proto. 3 otherwise.

If you subtract from top's defense the 5 they don't want to lose, you are left with 14. If bottom doesn't freeze a proto, they threaten 13, so top defends. If bottom freezes a proto, they threaten 14, so in top's case the "14" defense they arrived at by subtracting the units they don't want to lose, would incorrectly indicate a breach.

So my question is, do you really have to consider each of the freeze options that involve the units you don't want to lose to correctly determine defense or is there some sort of shortcut?
[2018-02-25 13:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): -
I want a generic approach that is efficient
[2018-02-25 13:49] punf: if you dont want to lose something, its not defending
[2018-02-25 13:49] punf: i dont see the fucking issue here
[2018-02-25 13:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Then read my paragraph
[2018-02-25 13:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Top subtacts 5 from 19 = 14
[2018-02-25 13:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Bottom threatens 14 by freezing one of the "not defending shit"
[2018-02-25 13:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Your subtracted result indicates a breach, even though there isn't breach
[2018-02-25 13:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): How to get around this
[2018-02-25 13:51] punf: it turns out that my method generates the correct decision in that scenario
[2018-02-25 13:51] punf: (obv its correct to click the drone)
[2018-02-25 13:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): When you say "not defending" that counts for the freeze options as well
[2018-02-25 13:51] punf: yes. this is particularly convenient in the 1hp colossus case since, you know, you can actually click it
[2018-02-25 13:52] punf: it also works for 1hp proto provided you remember to unclick it
[2018-02-25 13:52] mtanzer: the protos with less than 4 hp were not blockers
[2018-02-25 13:52] mtanzer: that's what punf is saying
[2018-02-25 13:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I just didn't realize that when freeze is in the mix, you can ignore the freeze options that involve the units you don't wanna lose

[2018-02-26 12:46] punf: ok, would you care to list some equally nontrivial observations elyot has made which were right?
[2018-02-26 12:48] 307th: people already have... plus the stuff on chill and gambits
[2018-02-26 12:50] 307th: not to mention, i think he was right that the flame animus + shadowfang combo wasn't an autowin
[2018-02-26 12:51] 307th: because he showed that someone who'd analyzed the set could win as p2 vs. someone playing p1 who hadn't studied the set and just did hte obvious build
[2018-02-26 12:51] crash_overlord: It ended up being, but it wasnt as simple as people thought it was
[2018-02-26 12:51] 307th: imo autowin doesn't mean "if you analyze the set enough, one player wins" - it means "there's an obvious line that always wins"
[2018-02-26 12:51] 307th: if the obvious line loses, it's not an autowin set
[2018-02-26 12:51] punf: 'you should not defend fully vs. chill' isnt a very insightful claim, so you receive few points for making it and it being correct
[2018-02-26 12:51] crash_overlord: It was very complicated because the green from p2 was pretty good. But p1 had a way to respond to win and p2 couldn't do anything against it. Which is why in the end flame animus got nerfed
[2018-02-26 12:52] 307th: he went in-depth about how to decide how much to defend vs. chill
[2018-02-26 12:52] awaclus: technically there's probably an "if you analyze the set enough, one player wins" in every set
[2018-02-26 12:52] 307th: and talked about leaving your opponent indifferent in their decision
[2018-02-26 12:52] 307th: which is pretty high level strategy especially given that it was several years ago
[2018-02-26 12:52] punf: what he didnt go in-depth. he said 'its nontrivial. think hard. k cool'
[2018-02-26 12:52] 307th: the stuff about rush timings was also very insightful
[2018-02-26 12:52] crash_overlord: Yeah, but flame animus shadowfang was analyzable in ~30min ish
[2018-02-26 12:53] punf: its not like he said 'against X attack and Y chill, defend for f(X,Y). here is a formula for the function f'
[2018-02-26 12:53] crash_overlord: Which makes it a loooooooot worse most sets
[2018-02-26 12:55] 307th: http://blog.prismata.net/2015/07/14/defending-against-frostbites-in-prismata/
[2018-02-26 12:55] punf: you get points for making testable predictions that are right, and lose points for making testable predictions that are wrong
[2018-02-26 12:56] punf: you dont get points for saying 'it is hard to decide how much to defend'
[2018-02-26 12:56] 307th: what? we're talking about strategy
[2018-02-26 12:56] punf: strategy comes in the form of predictions
[2018-02-26 12:56] punf: if i say 'absorb is very strong', that is a prediction of the form 'buying absorb wins more games than not buying absorb'
[2018-02-26 12:56] punf: if it then turns out that no, people who buy absorb usually lose, then my prediction was wrong and i lose points
[2018-02-26 12:57] 307th: if we're talking about predictions, he makes the prediction: "An optimal defensive play should make the attacker’s decision hard." about defending vs. frostbites
[2018-02-26 12:58] 307th: and that's definitely true
[2018-02-26 12:58] punf: yes that is a prediction. but the problem is that its quite a trivial prediction, so you get fairly few points for making it
[2018-02-26 12:58] 307th: your predictions are stuff like "always buy frostbite"
[2018-02-26 12:58] 307th: and then you're wrong
[2018-02-26 12:58] 307th: it's easy to sit on the sidelines 2 years later and say "all his predictions are trivial lol"
[2018-02-26 12:59] punf: yes ok it was less trivial at the time it was published so he should be given credit for that
[2018-02-26 13:00] punf: i dont think its a bad article at all
[2018-02-26 13:01] 307th: the other thing is, i think your focus on predictions is off
[2018-02-26 13:01] 307th: it puts all the focus on throwaway comments like 'arka sodara sure is OP'
[2018-02-26 13:01] punf: please give a piece of strategic advice that you believe to be useful that does not contain a prediction
[2018-02-26 13:01] 307th: yeah, but you're equivocating here
[2018-02-26 13:01] 307th: there's the really vague form of prediction
[2018-02-26 13:02] 307th: so you can say about any piece of strategy advice, "that's a prediction, because you're predicting that if you follow that advice you'll win more"
[2018-02-26 13:02] 307th: then there's the usual form of prediction which is "he said X unit is fine, but it's actually OP"
[2018-02-26 13:02] punf: you could say 'drone is a unit that costs 3e and produces 1 gold per turn'
[2018-02-26 13:02] punf: thats arguably 'strategic advice', and it definitely contains no prediction
[2018-02-26 13:03] punf: at any rate the wiki's strategy articles are filled with such 'strategic advice' as that
[2018-02-26 13:03] 307th: if you read my article 'the standard style', you won't find any specific predictions in there
[2018-02-26 13:04] 307th: just general advice, which you can technically turn into a prediction by saying "well he's predicting if you do that stuff you'll win"
[2018-02-26 13:04] punf: for what its worth i think that its a good article except for the part where its been written 20 times before
[2018-02-26 13:04] punf: ideally you would have a betting market for strategy advice
[2018-02-26 13:04] punf: maybe i think that absorb isnt as good as you say. so say, i will bet you $10 that i will play 100 games against you where i promise never to buy an absorber, and i win iff i win >30 games
[2018-02-26 13:05] punf: thus transforming the 'strategy' of words that anybody can write into an actual testable prediction
[2018-02-26 13:06] 307th: i'm surprised you want to focus on predictions
